code: *_____________________________________________________________________; * C G M F I L E S F O R P O W E R P O I N T S L I D E S *_____________________________________________________________________ * Competing risks , parametric only ; filename gsasfile "&STUDY/graphs/ce.states.ST.cgm "; %plot(goptions gsfmode =replace , device =cgmmppa , ftext=hwcgm001 , end;   axisx order =(0 to 5 by 1), minor=none , value =( height =2.4) , end;   axisy order =(0 to 100 by 20) , minor=none , value =( height =2.4) ,     value =( height =2.4 j=r ' ' '20 ' '40' '60 ' '80 ' '100 '), end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=noinit , width =3, color=gray , end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=nostrk , width =3, color=red , end;   tuple set=all , x=years , y=nodeath , width =3, color=blue , end; ); run; # Install the latest **hvtiPlotR** package. # # # The devtools package is installed on all our # jjnb-gen servers as well as other R instances. # For working on your own install, first use the install command # install.packages("devtools") # Load the package library("devtools") # To get the latest version of hvtiPlotR. install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR") library("foreign") # The xport file name dtFilename = "../datasets/par_cst.xpt" # Read the xport file into a data.frame dta<- read.xport(dtFilename) # Reading in the labels takes 2 more commands. dta.info <- lookup.xport(dtFilename) dta.labels <- dta.info[[1]]$label ## Fill in empty labels with the variable name dta.labels<-sapply(1:length(dta.labels), function(ind){ if(dta.labels[ind] == "")   colnames(dta)[ind] else   dta.labels[ind] }) ## For indexing the labels, names(dta.labels) = colnames(dta) # load required libraries library("ggplot2") # Plotting environment if (requireNamespace("hvtiPlotR", quietly = TRUE)) {   library("hvtiPlotR") # Use installed package when available } else {   pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE, helpers = FALSE, quiet = TRUE) }  # Load the example datasets data(parametric, package = "hvtiPlotR") data(nonparametric, package = "hvtiPlotR")  # Set a default hvtiPlotR plotting theme theme_set(hvtiPlotR::hvti_theme("manuscript")) ## To reproduce the plot.sas function, line by line. ###------------- ## There are SAS options we will not use here. # # %plot(goptions gsfmode=replace, device=pscolor, gaccess=gsasfile end; ccf_plot <- ggplot() ###------------- ## Labels are a single command, scales control the axis # # labelx l="Years After Randomization", end; # labely l="Percent in Each Category (ST)", end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   labs(x = "Years After Randomization", y = "Percent in Each Category (ST)") ###------------- ## Labels are a single command, scales control the axis # # axisx order=(0 to 5 by 1), minor=none, end; # axisy order=(0 to 100 by 10), minor=none, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 10)) ###------------- ## /******NON-PARAMETRIC: SYMBOLS AND CONFIDENCE BARS *******/ ## ## Each tuple statement corresponds to one or more geom_ statements # tuple set=green, symbol=dot, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sginit, cll=stlinit, clu=stuinit, color=black, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(data = nonparametric, aes(x = iv_state, y = sginit))  show(ccf_plot) # tuple set=green, symbol=circle, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sgdead1, cll=stldead1, clu=studead1, color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, y = sgdead1),     color = "blue",     shape = 1   ) +   geom_errorbar(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, ymin = stldead1, ymax = studead1),     color = "blue",     width = .1   ) # tuple set=green, symbol=square, symbsize=1/2, linepe=0, linecl=0, # ebarsize=3/4, ebar=1, # x=iv_state, y=sgstrk1, cll=stlstrk1, clu=stustrk1, color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_point(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, y = sgstrk1),     color = "blue",     shape = 0   ) +   geom_errorbar(     data = nonparametric,     aes(x = iv_state, ymin = stlstrk1, ymax = stustrk1),     color = "blue",     width = .1   ) show(ccf_plot) Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). Warning: Removed 117 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). # /**********PARAMETRIC : SOLID LINES AND CONFIDENCE INTERVALS**********/ # tuple set=all, x=years, y=noinit, cll=clinit, clu=cuinit, # width=0.5,color=black, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_line(data = parametric, aes(x = years, y = noinit)) +   geom_line(data = parametric, aes(x = years, y = clinit), linetype = "dashed") +   geom_line(data = parametric, aes(x = years, y = cuinit), linetype = "dashed") # # tuple set=all, x=years, y=nodeath, cll=cldeath, clu=cudeath, # width=0.5,color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_line(data = parametric,             aes(x = years, y = nodeath),             color = "blue") +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = cldeath),     linetype = "dashed",     color = "blue"   ) +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = cudeath),     linetype = "dashed",     color = "blue"   ) # # tuple set=all, x=years, y=nostrk, cll=clstrk, clu=custrk, # linecl=2, width=0.5,color=blue, end; ccf_plot <- ccf_plot +   geom_line(data = parametric,             aes(x = years, y = nostrk),             color = "blue") +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = clstrk),     linetype = "dashed",     color = "blue"   ) +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = custrk),     linetype = "dashed",     color = "blue"   ) show(ccf_plot) Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). Warning: Removed 117 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). # %plot(goptions gsfmode=replace, device=cgmmppa, ftext=hwcgm001, end; #   axisx order=(0 to 5 by 1), minor=none, value=(height=2.4), end; #   axisy order=(0 to 100 by 20), minor=none, value=(height=2.4), #   value=(height=2.4 j=r 20 40 60 80 100 ), end; #   tuple set=all, x=years, y=noinit, width=3, color=gray, end; #   tuple set=all, x=years, y=nostrk, width=3, color=red, end; #   tuple set=all, x=years, y=nodeath, width=3, color=blue, end; # ); ccf_pptPlot <- ggplot() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = noinit),     color = "grey",     size = 1.5   ) +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = nostrk),     color = "red",     size = 1.5   ) +   geom_line(     data = parametric,     aes(x = years, y = nodeath),     color = "blue",     size = 1.5   ) Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. show(ccf_pptPlot)

text: common figure propensity-matched analyses mirrored histogram, displays propensity score distributions two treatment groups matching. hvtiPlotR package provides mirror_histogram() function generate figure. function accepts data frame columns propensity score, group indicator, match indicator. sample_mirror_histogram_data() function generates example data suitable testing.  lighter bars show full (pre-match) propensity score distribution group, darker overlaid bars show matched subset. upper panel corresponds first group label lower panel second. function also returns diagnostics summarising group counts standardized mean differences (SMD) matching:
code: # Generate sample data for the mirrored histogram mirror_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 2000)  # Generate the mirrored histogram mhist <- mirror_histogram(   data = mirror_dta,   score_col = "prob_t",   group_col = "tavr",   match_col = "match",   group_levels = c(0, 1),   group_labels = c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR"),   matched_value = 1,   score_multiplier = 100,   binwidth = 5,   alpha = 0.8 )  # Display the plot mhist$plot # Standardized mean difference before matching mhist$diagnostics$smd_before [1] 1.52139 # Standardized mean difference after matching mhist$diagnostics$smd_matched [1] 0.02597089 # Group counts before matching mhist$diagnostics$group_counts_before 0    1 2000 2000 # Group counts after matching mhist$diagnostics$group_counts_matched 0   1 927 927

text: common exploratory figure stacked histogram, shows composition numeric variable changes time across another grouping dimension. hvtiPlotR package provides stacked_histogram() function generate figure. function returns bare ggplot object — colour scales, axis labels, theme applied — caller can add freely usual + operator. sample_stacked_histogram_data() function generates reproducible example dataset year category columns. default position = "stack" shows raw counts within bin, equivalent plot.sas frequency histogram.  Setting position = "fill" rescales bin bars sum 1, making easy compare relative composition across years.  Use ggsave() write composed figure disk. Assign final plot variable first object saved displayed.
code: # Generate sample data hist_dta <- sample_stacked_histogram_data(n_years = 20, start_year = 2000,                                            n_categories = 3) head(hist_dta) year category 1 2000        1 2 2000        1 3 2000        2 4 2000        2 5 2000        2 6 2000        1 # Build the bare plot p_count <- stacked_histogram(hist_dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category")  # Layer on colour scales, labels, and a theme p_count +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Count") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") # Build the proportional variant p_fill <- stacked_histogram(hist_dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category",                              position = "fill")  # Use manual colours and custom legend labels p_fill +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     labels = c("1" = "Group A", "2" = "Group B", "3" = "Group C"),     name   = "Category"   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") p_final <- p_fill +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     name   = "Category"   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("1" = "pink", "2" = "cyan", "3" = "orangered"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Proportion") +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/stacked_histogram.pdf",   plot     = p_final,   width    = 11,   height   = 8 )

text: goodness--follow-plot standard quality-control figure longitudinal outcome analyses. displays patient point operation date (x-axis) follow-duration (y-axis), short vertical tick point. dashed diagonal line marks maximum potential follow-given study start, study end, follow-closing date. hvtiPlotR package provides goodness_followup() build figure. function returns bare ggplot object colour, shape, axis, label scales applied — added caller standard ggplot2 modifiers.  Scale, label, annotation layers composed usual + operator. scale_color_manual() scale_shape_manual() map binary alive/dead state colours point shapes. annotate() places group-identifying text directly panel.  diagonal dashed line represents maximum potential follow-. Points sitting line indicate patients longer follow-expected study window, typically due passive surveillance supplementing active cross-sectional follow-. Assign fully composed plot variable, pass ggsave(). dataset includes non-fatal competing event (e.g. relapse, reoperation), pass event_col, event_time_col, optionally death_for_event_col generate second panel alongside death panel. follow-time event panel typically restricted active/systematic follow-window, using separate death indicator (deads) rather -source death indicator (dead). state factor event panel three levels: event — alive event-free censoring Non-fatal event — event occurred first Death — patient died event  death panel (gfup2$death_plot) event panel (gfup2$event_plot) share diagonal reference line can saved individually ggsave().
code: gfup_dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data(n = 300, seed = 42) head(gfup_dta) iv_opyrs iv_dead  dead iv_event ev_event deads 1  29.5694  2.0261 FALSE   2.0261    FALSE FALSE 2   6.4834  6.9027  TRUE   4.0813     TRUE  TRUE 3  14.4342  5.4468  TRUE   5.4468    FALSE  TRUE 4  25.4324  6.1630 FALSE   0.6137     TRUE FALSE 5   3.4251 13.1369  TRUE   6.9232     TRUE  TRUE 6  24.1620  7.4334 FALSE   7.4334    FALSE FALSE gfup <- goodness_followup(   data        = gfup_dta,   origin_year = 1990,   study_start = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end   = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date  = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   alpha       = 0.8 )  # Bare plot — no scales or labels yet gfup$death_plot library(RColorBrewer)  gfup$death_plot +   # Colour alive = blue, dead = red (Set1 palette positions 2 and 1)   scale_color_manual(     values   = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[c(2, 1)],     labels   = c("Alive", "Dead"),     na.value = "black",     drop     = FALSE   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c(1, 4),     labels = c("Alive", "Dead")   ) +   # Axis tick placement   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2020, 3)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 33,   3)) +   # Clip the panel to the study window   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 33), xlim = c(1990, 2020)) +   # Axis and legend labels   labs(     x     = "Operation Date",     y     = "Follow-up (years)",     color = "Status",     shape = "Status"   ) +   # Annotate directly on the panel   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 31, label = "Alive at close",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 28, label = "Deceased",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[1]) +   theme(legend.position = "none") gfup_final <- gfup$death_plot +   scale_color_manual(     values   = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[c(2, 1)],     labels   = c("Alive", "Dead"),     na.value = "black",     drop     = FALSE   ) +   scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 4), labels = c("Alive", "Dead")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1990, 2020, 3)) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 33, 3)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 33), xlim = c(1990, 2020)) +   labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)",        color = "Status", shape = "Status") +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 31, label = "Alive at close",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +   annotate("text", x = 1993, y = 28, label = "Deceased",            hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = brewer.pal(3, "Set1")[1]) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/dp_goodness-of-followup.pdf",   plot     = gfup_final,   height   = 6,   width    = 6 ) gfup_event_dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data(n = 300, seed = 42) gfup2 <- goodness_followup(   gfup_event_dta,   origin_year         = 1990, "iv_event",   death_for_event_col = "deads",   event_levels        = c("No event", "Relapse", "Death"),   alpha               = 0.8 )  gfup2$event_plot +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("No event" = "blue", "Relapse" = "green3", "Death" = "red"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("No event" = 1L, "Relapse" = 2L, "Death" = 4L),     name   = NULL   ) + title: The hvtiPlotR package: Generating plot.sas style in R
text: covariate balance plot standard quality-control figure propensity score matching IPTW analyses. covariate occupies labelled row; points show standardized mean difference (SMD) comparison group (e.g. matching). solid vertical line marks zero balance; dotted vertical lines mark imbalance threshold (default ±10%). hvtiPlotR package provides covariate_balance() build figure. returns bare ggplot object — colour, shape, axis labels, theme applied — styling added usual + operator. Input data must long format: one row per covariate × group combination columns covariate name, group label, numeric SMD value.  scale_color_manual() scale_shape_manual() assign visual encodings two comparison groups. scale_x_continuous() sets axis range tick positions.  annotate() places explanatory text directly panel indicate direction imbalance favours group.  Points sitting beyond dotted ±10% threshold lines indicate covariates remain imbalanced matching may warrant investigation sensitivity analyses. Pass var_levels control bottom--top display order covariates.
code: dta_cb <- sample_covariate_balance_data(n_vars = 12) head(dta_cb) variable        group std_diff 1               Age Before match      9.8 2        Female sex Before match     25.4 3      Hypertension Before match    -14.7 4 Diabetes mellitus Before match     -8.9 5              COPD Before match     -3.9 6        Creatinine Before match     26.5 covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) library(RColorBrewer)  covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(-45, 35),     breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)   ) +   labs(     x = "Standardized difference (%)",     y = ""   ) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.95)) Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). n_vars <- length(unique(dta_cb$variable))  covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(-45, 35),     breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)   ) +   labs(     x = "Standardized difference: Group A \u2013 Group B (%)",     y = ""   ) +   # Directional labels at fixed panel positions   annotate("text", x = -32, y = 0.4,        label = "More likely Group B", size = 4) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = n_vars + 1, label = "More likely Group A", size = 4) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.95)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript") Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range (`geom_point()`). # Reverse the default order so the first covariate appears at the top covariate_balance(   dta_cb,   var_levels = rev(unique(dta_cb$variable)),   alpha      = 0.8 ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference (%)", y = "") +   hvti_theme("manuscript") cb_final <- covariate_balance(dta_cb, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-45, 35), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference (%)", y = "") +   annotate("text", x = -32, y = 0.4,        label = "More likely Group B", size = 4) +   annotate("text", x =  22, y = n_vars + 1, label = "More likely Group A", size = 4) +   theme(legend.position = c(0.20, 0.95)) +   hvti_theme("manuscript")  ggsave(   filename = "../graphs/lp_cov-balance.pdf",   plot     = cb_final,   height   = 7,   width    = 8 )

text: hvtiPlotR R port plot.sas macro suite used Clinical Investigations Statistics group within Heart & Vascular Institute Cleveland Clinic. produces publication-quality graphics conform HVI manuscript presentation standards using ggplot2::ggplot2 officer::officer package. plot functions return bare ggplot2::ggplot() objects (lists ) callers can apply additional ggplot2 layers, scales, themes without restriction.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: hvti_theme(): Unified theme dispatcher ("manuscript", "ppt", "dark_ppt", "poster"). theme_man() / theme_manuscript: Theme manuscript figures. theme_ppt(): Theme PowerPoint presentation figures. theme_dark_ppt(): Dark theme PowerPoint presentations. theme_poster(): Theme poster figures.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: save_ppt(): Save ggplot objects PowerPoint presentation via officer::officer package. makeFootnote(): Add footnotes graphics.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: hvti_plot(): Single entry-point dispatcher hvtiPlotR plot types ("mirror_histogram", "stacked_histogram", "covariate_balance"). mirror_histogram(): Side--side propensity-score histograms (binary-match IPTW-weighted mode). Ports plot.sas mirror histogram output. stacked_histogram(): Stacked (filled) histogram numeric variable faceted grouping factor. covariate_balance(): Dot-plot standardised mean differences propensity-score matching weighting. goodness_followup(): Goodness--follow-scatter plot showing actual vs. potential follow-per operation year. Optionally includes non-fatal-event panel. survival_curve(): Kaplan-Meier Nelson-Aalen survival analysis returning five plot types (survival, cumulative hazard, hazard, log-log, life/RMST) plus risk report tables. Ports SAS %kaplan %nelsont macros tp.ac.dead.sas.

title: hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts — hvtiPlotR-package
text: plot function ships companion generator use examples tests: sample_mirror_histogram_data(): Simulates propensity scores via logistic model greedy 1:1 caliper matching optional IPTW weights. sample_stacked_histogram_data(): Simulates year--category count data. sample_covariate_balance_data(): Simulates patient-level covariates logistic propensity model caliper matching, returning standardised mean differences matching. sample_goodness_followup_data(): Simulates operative cohort operation dates, follow-times, death, non-fatal events. sample_survival_data(): Simulates exponential survival times administrative censoring optional treatment strata.

text: cumhaz Cumulative hazard H(t) = -log S(t). log_cumhaz log H(t) — y-axis log-log survival plot used assess proportional-hazards assumption. log_time log(t) — x-axis log-scale PLOTC plots. hazard Instantaneous hazard estimate: log(S(t_prev) / S(t)) / (t - t_prev).  defined event times delta_t > 0; NA censoring times. density Probability density: (S(t_prev) - S(t)) / delta_t. mid_time Midpoint interval (t_prev, t], used x-axis hazard-rate plots. life Cumulative integral survival function (restricted mean survival time) using SAS trapezoidal rule: LIFE += delta_t * (3*S(t) - S(t_prev)) / 2. proplife LIFE / t — proportionate life length.

title: Plot Mirrored Propensity Score Histogram — mirror_histogram
text: data data frame containing least score group columns, plus either match_col weight_col. score_col Column name holding numeric propensity score. group_col Column name identifying grouping/treatment indicator. match_col Column name binary match indicator. Default "match". Required binary-match mode; ignored weight_col supplied. group_levels Length-2 vector giving values group_col plot (order determines panel orientation). group_labels Length-2 character vector human-readable group labels. matched_value Value match_col denotes matched observation (binary-match mode ). score_multiplier Multiplier applied score_col plotting (default scales raw probabilities percentages). binwidth Bin width scaled score scale. weight_col Optional column name holding IPTW weights. supplied function operates weighted mode: "" bars show raw counts, "Weighted" bars show per-bin weight sums, match_col required. column must numeric non-negative. alpha Transparency histogram bars, [0, 1]. Default 0.8. output_file Optional file path; provided plot saved via ggsave(). width, height Dimensions (inches) saving output_file.

title: Plot Mirrored Propensity Score Histogram — mirror_histogram
text: list elements plot (ggplot object), diagnostics (mode-dependent summary statistics), data (filtered working data frame). Binary-match diagnostics include smd_matched; weighted diagnostics include smd_weighted effective_n_by_group.

title: Plot Mirrored Propensity Score Histogram — mirror_histogram
text: Binary-match mode Supply match_col. Upper bars show observations (matching); overlaid bars show matched subset. Fill keys: before_g0, matched_g0, before_g1, matched_g1. Weighted IPTW mode Supply weight_col. Upper bars show raw counts (weighting); overlaid bars show per-bin weight sums. match_col ignored. Fill keys: before_g0, weighted_g0, before_g1, weighted_g1.

title: Plot Mirrored Propensity Score Histogram — mirror_histogram
code: # --- Binary-match mode --------------------------------------------------- # separation = 1.5 leaves many high/low-score patients unmatched at tails mirror_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 500, separation = 1.5) mhist <- mirror_histogram(mirror_dta, alpha = 0.8) mhist$plot  mhist$diagnostics$smd_before #> [1] 1.524308 mhist$diagnostics$smd_matched #> [1] 0.02732008  # Customise fill colours mhist$plot +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(before_g0 = "white",  matched_g0 = "steelblue",                before_g1 = "white",  matched_g1 = "firebrick"),     guide = "none"   )   # --- Weighted IPTW mode -------------------------------------------------- wt_dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 500, add_weights = TRUE) mhist_wt <- mirror_histogram(wt_dta, weight_col = "mt_wt", alpha = 0.8) mhist_wt$plot  mhist_wt$diagnostics$smd_weighted #> [1] 0.5483862 mhist_wt$diagnostics$effective_n_by_group #>   0   1  #> 500 500   # Customise fill colours for weighted mode mhist_wt$plot +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(before_g0 = "white", weighted_g0 = "blue",                before_g1 = "white", weighted_g1 = "red"),     guide = "none"   )

what: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
text: Produces reproducible long-format data frame suitable testing demonstrating covariate_balance().  Rather drawing SMDs independent normals, generator simulates patient-level covariates logistic propensity score model, computes group standardized mean differences matching, performs greedy 1:1 nearest-neighbour caliper matching computes residual differences matched cohort.

title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
code: sample_covariate_balance_data(   n_vars = 12,   n = 600,   separation = 1.5,   caliper = 0.05,   group_levels = c("Before match", "After match"),   seed = 42 )

title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
text: n_vars Integer. Number covariates generate. Default 12. n Integer. Total number simulated patients matching. Default 600 (roughly 300 per group separation = 1.5). separation Numeric. Distance two group means log-odds scale.  Larger values push propensity score distributions apart, increasing proportion unmatched extreme-score patients residual imbalance matching.  Default 1.5. caliper Matching caliper expressed propensity-score units (0–1 scale).  Patients without partner within distance left unmatched.  Default 0.05. group_levels Length-2 character vector group labels. Default c("match", "match"). seed Integer random seed reproducibility. Default 42.

title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
text: data frame 2 * n_vars rows columns variable, group, std_diff (standardized mean difference percentage).

title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
text: result captures pattern seen real studies: covariates drive treatment selection show large imbalance matching; matching substantially reduces imbalance, patients propensity score extremes matched, leaving small residual differences strongest confounders.

title: Generate Sample Covariate Balance Data — sample_covariate_balance_data
code: dta <- sample_covariate_balance_data() head(dta) #>            variable        group std_diff #> 1               Age Before match      9.8 #> 2        Female sex Before match     25.4 #> 3      Hypertension Before match    -14.7 #> 4 Diabetes mellitus Before match     -8.9 #> 5              COPD Before match     -3.9 #> 6        Creatinine Before match     26.5  # Higher separation -> more unmatched extremes -> more residual imbalance dta2 <- sample_covariate_balance_data(   n_vars       = 8,   separation   = 2.0,   group_levels = c("Unweighted", "IPTW weighted") )

what: Generate Sample Data for Mirrored Histogram — sample_mirror_histogram_data
title: Generate Sample Data for Mirrored Histogram — sample_mirror_histogram_data
text: Creates reproducible data frame testing mirror_histogram either binary-match weighted IPTW mode.  Propensity scores simulated via logistic model: control subjects draw linear predictor \(N(-\text{sep}/2, 1)\) treated subjects \(N(+\text{sep}/2, 1)\), two score distributions overlap centre accumulating mass opposite extremes.  Patients extremes find matching partner within caliper, naturally reproduces "many unmatched tails" pattern seen real studies.

title: Generate Sample Data for Mirrored Histogram — sample_mirror_histogram_data
code: sample_mirror_histogram_data(   n = 500,   separation = 1.5,   caliper = 0.05,   seed = 123,   add_weights = FALSE )

title: Generate Sample Data for Mirrored Histogram — sample_mirror_histogram_data
text: n Number observations per group (default 500). separation Numeric. Distance two group means log-odds scale.  Larger values push score distributions apart increase proportion unmatched patients extremes (default 1.5). caliper Matching caliper width expressed propensity-score units (0–1 scale, default 0.05).  Treated patients without control partner within distance left unmatched. seed Integer random seed reproducibility (default 123). add_weights Logical. TRUE mt_wt column ATE-style IPTW weights derived simulated propensity scores appended normalised mean 1 within group (default FALSE).

title: Generate Sample Data for Mirrored Histogram — sample_mirror_histogram_data
text: Data frame columns: prob_t Propensity score 0–1 scale. tavr Group indicator (0 = control, 1 = treated). match Binary match indicator produced greedy nearest-neighbour matching within caliper (1 = matched). mt_wt (add_weights = TRUE) ATE IPTW weights normalised mean 1 within group.

title: Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve — survival_curve
text: Estimates Kaplan-Meier survival function returns list containing five bare ggplot objects (survival curve, cumulative hazard, hazard rate, log-minus-log survival, mean residual life), plus associated data frames (tidy KM data, numbers--risk table, report table specified time points). returned plots intentionally omit scale, label, theme modifications caller can layer choices +.

title: Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve — survival_curve
code: survival_curve(   data,   time_col = "iv_dead",   event_col = "dead",   strata_col = NULL,   conf_int = TRUE,   conf_level = 0.6827,   report_times = c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25),   alpha = 0.8,   method = c("kaplan-meier", "nelson-aalen") )

title: Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve — survival_curve
text: data data frame. time_col Name numeric column holding follow-time years. Defaults "iv_dead". event_col Name logical 0/1 column indicating whether event occurred. Defaults "dead". strata_col Optional name character factor column used stratify analysis. Pass NULL (default) unstratified estimate. conf_int Logical; draw confidence-interval ribbon survival plot. Defaults TRUE. conf_level Confidence level CI band. Defaults 0.6827 (one standard deviation), matching SAS %kaplan macro default. report_times Numeric vector time points survival estimates numbers risk reported. Defaults c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25). alpha Transparency plot lines points, [0, 1]. Default 0.8. CI ribbon uses fixed transparency 0.2. method Estimator use.  "kaplan-meier" (default) computes product-limit estimate logit CI — corresponding SAS %kaplan macro.  "nelson-aalen" uses Fleming-Harrington cumulative hazard \(H(t) = \sum d_i / n_i\) \(S(t) = \exp(-H)\) log CI \(H(t)\) — corresponding SAS %nelsont macro, preferred \(S(t)\) falls near zero.

title: Kaplan-Meier Survival Curve — survival_curve
text: named list elements mirroring SAS %kaplan macro output plots (PLOTS, PLOTC, PLOTH, PLOTL): survival_plot (PLOTS=1) Bare ggplot: KM step function optional CI ribbon, y-axis 0–100 scale. cumhaz_plot (PLOTC=1) Bare ggplot: Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard H(t) = -log S(t). hazard_plot (PLOTH=1) Bare ggplot: instantaneous hazard h(t) = log(S(t_prev)/S(t)) / delta_t, plotted interval midpoints.  Add geom_smooth(method="loess") smoothed hazard curve. loglog_plot (PLOTC=1, log-log variant) Bare ggplot: log H(t) vs log t.  Parallel lines across strata indicate proportional hazards. life_plot (PLOTL=1) Bare ggplot: restricted mean survival time (integral S(t)) vs time. km_data Tidy data frame columns time, surv, lower, upper, n.risk, n.event, n.censor, cumhaz, strata, hazard, density, mid_time, life, proplife, log_cumhaz, log_time. risk_table Data frame: strata, report_time, n.risk. report_table Data frame: strata, report_time, surv, lower, upper, n.risk, n.event.

title: hvtiPlotR 1.1.0
text: Added survival_curve() — Kaplan-Meier Nelson-Aalen survival analysis returning five plot types (survival, cumulative hazard, hazard, log-log, life/RMST) plus risk report tables. Ports SAS %kaplan %nelsont macros tp.ac.dead.sas. Added sample_survival_data() — realistic exponential survival simulation administrative censoring optional treatment strata. Added goodness_followup() — goodness--follow-scatter plot showing actual vs. potential follow-per operation year, optional non-fatal event panel. Added sample_goodness_followup_data() — simulates operative cohort operation dates, follow-times, competing events, death. Added covariate_balance() — standardised mean difference dot-plot propensity-score matching weighting diagnostics. Added sample_covariate_balance_data() — patient-level logistic simulation greedy 1:1 caliper matching; SMDs computed matching. Added stacked_histogram() sample_stacked_histogram_data() — stacked filled histogram numeric variable group. Improved mirror_histogram() sample data (sample_mirror_histogram_data()) use realistic logistic propensity-score model greedy 1:1 caliper matching optional ATE IPTW weights; extreme-PS patients naturally go unmatched. Added hvti_plot() dispatcher supporting "mirror_histogram", "stacked_histogram", "covariate_balance" plot types. Added hvti_theme() dispatcher "manuscript", "ppt", "dark_ppt", "poster" themes. Enabled roxygen Markdown (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) **bold**, backtick code spans, [pkg::fn()] cross-references render correctly Rd help pages. Added survival package Imports. Updated package-level documentation (help.R) reflect current exported functions sample-data generators.
